summary: US spot crypto ETFs route traditional brokerage money into BTC and ETH; the daily net flow figure (published by Farside/SoSoValue) has become a primary demand gauge. Three repeatable patterns emerge: ETF launch days tend to "sell the news" (a known catalyst is bought into and dumped on the event), sustained multi-week net inflows are reliably bullish while sustained net outflows are bearish (ETFs are a marginal-buyer proxy), and one-time structural outflows from a converted high-fee legacy vehicle (GBTC, ETHE) create mechanical sell pressure independent of sentiment. Flow direction leads or coincides with spot price, making it a strong, computable backtest feature.

archetypes.name: Spot ETF launch day / approval 'sell the news'
archetypes.mechanism: A spot ETF launch is a heavily pre-announced, date-certain catalyst. Traders accumulate spot in the weeks before, pricing in the expected demand. On the actual launch the catalyst is realized, anticipatory longs and arbitrage/event traders take profit, and day-one ETF inflows (while large in headline trading volume) are partly offset by churn and by sellers of the legacy converted trust. Net result is a short-lived local top followed by a 1-2 week drawdown. The buyers who 'lose' are the late-cycle longs who bought the rumor at the highs and the retail crowd chasing the launch headline; the sellers are early accumulators and arbitrageurs unwinding the now-closed discount.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate to hours; full drawdown plays out over days
archetypes.typical_duration: 1-2 weeks to the local trough, then recovery
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: -7% to -20% on the launching asset over 1-2 weeks
archetypes.recurrence: fires once per major product launch (BTC Jan 2024, ETH Jul 2024, HK Apr 2024); a recurring template for every future first-of-kind spot ETF launch (e.g. SOL, XRP)
leading_signals: asset rallies 20-60% in the weeks INTO the approval/launch date
perpetual funding rates elevated / positive heading into launch
options skew bid for calls into the event date
legacy trust (GBTC/ETHE) discount to NAV has collapsed toward zero pre-launch (arbitrage primed to unwind)

historical_instances.trigger: Zcash (ZEC) +180% as the Grayscale Zcash Trust files to convert to a spot ETF and Multicoin discloses a large position.
historical_instances.description: ZEC surged ~+180% over 3 months (Mar 4 $222 -> Jun 4 $624; intra-window peak ~$670, +240% from the low) on the Grayscale Zcash Trust's S-3 filing to convert into a spot ETF plus Multicoin Capital disclosing a large position (+40% in 24h to ~$607). The pump came DESPITE a severe fundamental negative: the entire Electric Coin Co. team (CEO Josh Swihart) resigned in Jan 2026 over a governance battle. A clean case of ETF-conversion-anticipation + institutional accumulation overriding a leaderless-dev situation - the filing/flow bid dominates idiosyncratic fundamentals. (ETF not yet approved; this is the anticipation phase, with sell-the-news risk on the eventual decision.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Grayscale Zcash Trust S-3 ETF-conversion filing; Multicoin large position; ECC team walkout (Jan 2026) ignored by the bid.

historical_instances.trigger: US spot Solana ETFs launch (3rd crypto after BTC/ETH) - SOL then crashes ~57% over 4 months.
historical_instances.description: US spot SOL ETFs began trading Oct 28 2025 (Bitwise, Grayscale, VanEck, Fidelity, Franklin, 21Shares, Canary) - the 3rd asset after BTC/ETH to get a US spot ETF. Sell-the-news on the launch (SOL -3.5% over Oct 26-31), then a brutal -57.5% over the next 4 months ($198->$84 by late Feb 2026, low $77.7, -61% max DD) as memecoin volume collapsed and TVL fell 56%. Emphatic confirmation (like ETH's ETF) that a spot-ETF launch is NOT a price floor - structural access ≠ demand when the use-case fades. Cumulative SOL-ETF inflows passed ~$1.12B but couldn't offset the fundamental deterioration.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Cumulative SOL-ETF inflows ~$1.12B; SOL still crashed ~57% (memecoin volume collapse, TVL -56%).

historical_instances.trigger: First US spot Ethereum ETFs (ETHA, FETH, ETHE conversion, etc.) begin trading; >$1B day-one volume.
historical_instances.description: ETH sold off after launch as ETHE (Grayscale) outflows dominated early net flows and anticipatory longs exited. The decline was sharply compounded by the broad Aug 5 2024 yen carry-trade unwind crash, so the multi-week figure is not purely ETF-attributable.

price_impacts.window: 2024-07-22 close $3,449 to 2024-08-05 close $2,227 (incl. Aug 5 carry-trade crash)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: >$1B launch-day ETF volume; ETHE conversion outflows dominated early net flows

historical_instances.trigger: Asia's first spot BTC and ETH ETFs (China AMC, Bosera HashKey, Harvest) debut on HKEX with in-kind creation.
historical_instances.description: Hugely hyped but volumes disappointed badly (~$300-350M total AUM, <$14M first-week inflows vs billions in the US). No meaningful price catalyst; demonstrates that an ETF launch with weak real flows produces no sustained move.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$284M BTC ETF AUM + ~$66M ETH ETF AUM; <$14M first-week inflows — far below US

historical_instances.trigger: First 10-11 US spot Bitcoin ETFs (IBIT, FBTC, GBTC conversion, ARKB, etc.) begin trading; SEC approved Jan 10. ~$4.6B first-day trading volume.
historical_instances.description: Classic sell-the-news: BTC had rallied into the approval, peaked ~$46-49k, then sold off as the catalyst realized and GBTC outflows began. Local top marked on the event.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$4.6B day-one ETF trading volume; GBTC outflows began immediately (~$5.9B left GBTC by end of Jan)

archetypes.name: Sustained daily net inflows (bullish) / net outflows (bearish)
archetypes.mechanism: Spot ETFs are a structural marginal buyer: every dollar of net inflow forces the authorized participant to buy spot BTC/ETH for creation, and every dollar of net outflow forces a redemption sale. Because ETF demand comes from RIAs, wealth platforms and 401k-adjacent money that does not trade intraday, multi-day/multi-week flow streaks are persistent and trend-reinforcing. Sustained inflows act as a dry-powder injection that lifts price; sustained outflows remove the marginal bid and amplify spot weakness. The losers in an outflow regime are leveraged longs who get liquidated as the structural bid vanishes; winners are those who read the flow tape early. Flow is reflexive — falling price triggers redemptions which push price lower.
archetypes.typical_lag: same-day to 1-3 days (flow tends to lead/coincide with price)
archetypes.typical_duration: as long as the streak persists — days to several weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: inflow streaks: +8% to +50% over the run; outflow streaks: -3% to -20% over the run
archetypes.recurrence: ongoing and continuous — flows are reported every trading day; multiple distinct streaks per year
leading_signals: consecutive-day net flow streak (sign and length)
single-day flow magnitude crossing prior records (>$1B in / >$500M out)
IBIT (BTC) / ETHA (ETH) flow dominance — the bellwether funds
flow divergence from price (flows turning while price flat = leading signal)

historical_instances.trigger: Spot ETH ETFs post a 10-day inflow streak (longest since the Jul-2024 launch) - a modest mechanical floor.
historical_instances.description: US spot ETH ETFs logged 10 consecutive inflow days through Apr 22 2026, the longest since launch, but MODEST in size (~$50-65M/day total; ETHA $53.6M, FETH $40.6M on the final day) - a mechanical price floor absorbing sell pressure, not 'explosive demand'. ETH bounced +9.41% over the streak. The bid did not reverse the trend: ETH still fell ~40% YTD (to ~$1,811 by Jun 4 2026) with ~$401M of ETH-ETF outflows in May. Lesson: ETF inflow streaks are a floor, not a driver, in a risk-off regime. (Distinct from the Aug-2025 record, which was a $2.85B WEEKLY inflow / $1.02B single-day, not a single-day $2.85B.)

price_impacts.window: Jan 2-Jun 4 2026 ($3,000->$1,811); inflows did not stop the slide

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$50-65M/day inflows (BlackRock ETHA/Fidelity FETH led); ~$401M ETH-ETF outflows in May 2026.

historical_instances.trigger: US spot BTC ETFs post an 8-day, $2.1B net-inflow streak (longest since the Oct-2025 9-day run).
historical_instances.description: An 8-day spot-BTC-ETF inflow streak totaling ~$2.1B (Apr 14-23 2026), part of April's $2.44B month (strongest of 2026, BlackRock ~70% of flows). Demand-supportive but modest: BTC rose +3.82% ($74.5k->$77.4k) — an inflow-driven bounce inside a broader 2026 downtrend, not an explosive move.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$2.1B over 8 days; April total $2.44B net inflow.

historical_instances.trigger: Record IBIT single-day outflow of $523.2M (Nov 18); IBIT November outflows ~$2.34-2.7B (worst month ever) amid ~30% pullback from October highs.
historical_instances.description: Largest sustained ETF outflow episode to date. Structural bid collapse compounded a -20%+ BTC drawdown. Measured directly on CoinGecko.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: IBIT record $523.2M one-day outflow Nov 18; ~$2.34-2.7B IBIT November outflows (record month)

historical_instances.trigger: A 9-day US spot BTC ETF inflow streak carries BTC to its $126,210 all-time high (Oct 6 2025).
historical_instances.description: A sustained 9-day spot-BTC-ETF net-inflow streak (the strongest run of 2025) drove BTC +12.4% over the window to its all-time high of $126,210 on 2025-10-06. Canonical 'sustained inflow regime = grind higher' instance; the cycle topped shortly after.

historical_instances.trigger: Spot ETH ETFs cross $1B daily net inflow for the first time ever (Aug 11, ETHA $639.8M, FETH $277M).
historical_instances.description: Record ETH ETF inflow day coincided with ETH ripping from ~$3,500 to ~$4,760. Best single example of inflow-driven momentum in ETH.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: First-ever >$1B single-day ETH ETF net inflow

historical_instances.trigger: Record ETH ETF inflow surge — ETHA largest daily inflow (>$300M), ~$700M weekly; July total $5.46B (+564% MoM).
historical_instances.description: ETF inflows the dominant driver of ETH's ~50% July rally. Direct CoinGecko measurement.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: July ETH ETF net inflows $5.46B, +564% vs June

historical_instances.trigger: Heavy sustained spot BTC ETF inflows through early-mid July 2025.
historical_instances.description: Inflow regime drove BTC to fresh highs above $120k. Measured directly on CoinGecko.

historical_instances.trigger: Worst-ever spot BTC ETF outflow streak at the time — ~$1.14B single-day outflow Feb 25, multi-billion over the week (consecutive outflow sessions).
historical_instances.description: Structural bid removed during macro risk-off; BTC slid from the high-$90k range to the high-$70k/low-$80k range. Confirms sustained outflows are bearish. (Note: original catalog had a Feb 24 2024 date typo and wrong start price; corrected to 2025 with verified prices.)
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$1.14B single-day outflow Feb 25 (record at the time); multi-billion weekly net outflow

historical_instances.trigger: Record single-day spot BTC ETF net inflow of $1.38B (IBIT $1.11B) amid the Trump election win; multi-week inflow streak.
historical_instances.description: Post-election dry-powder surge. Record inflows coincided with BTC's breakout toward $90k. Strongest demonstration that record inflow days mark accelerating uptrends.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Record $1.38B single-day net inflow; IBIT $1.11B in one day

archetypes.name: Converted legacy trust structural outflow (GBTC / ETHE)
archetypes.mechanism: When a high-fee closed-end trust (GBTC at 1.5%, ETHE at 2.5%) converts to an open-ended ETF, two mechanical sell forces fire: (1) arbitrageurs who bought the trust at a deep discount to NAV finally redeem for the now-realizable arbitrage profit, and (2) holders rotate to the new low-fee ETFs (IBIT/ETHA at ~0.2%). Each redemption forces the trust to sell underlying spot BTC/ETH on the market. This is supply hitting the market that is independent of sentiment — it is a fee-and-arbitrage-driven, mechanical, front-loaded outflow that decays over weeks as the discount-buyers are flushed out. The 'losers' are spot holders who eat the sell pressure; the trust holders cashing out are realizing a long-awaited arbitrage.
archetypes.typical_lag: begins day 1 of conversion; heaviest in first 2-4 weeks
archetypes.typical_duration: front-loaded over ~6-10 weeks then decays to equilibrium
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: contributed -15% to -20% peak-to-trough on BTC over the Jan-Feb 2024 episode; effect fades as outflows slow
archetypes.recurrence: once per converted trust (GBTC Jan 2024, ETHE Jul 2024); a template for any future closed-end-to-ETF conversion
leading_signals: trust discount-to-NAV collapsing toward 0% pre-conversion (signals imminent arbitrage unwind)
trust fee far above competing ETFs (fee gap drives rotation)
daily trust outflow magnitude and its decay rate (slowing = exhaustion / bottoming signal)
trust AUM as % of total category AUM

historical_instances.trigger: GBTC converts to spot ETF; ~$5.9B left GBTC in January alone, heavy daily forced selling.
historical_instances.description: The dominant bearish force in the post-launch correction. Arbitrageurs who bought GBTC at deep discounts redeemed en masse; BTC fell from ~$49k to ~$39.5k. Cumulative GBTC outflows reached ~$14.5B by March 2024 before slowing toward equilibrium by April.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$5.9B GBTC outflows in Jan 2024; ~$14.5B cumulative by March 2024; outflows slowed sharply by April 2024

historical_instances.trigger: ETHE (Grayscale Ethereum Trust, 2.5% fee) converts to spot ETF; heavy outflows dominate early net ETH ETF flows.
historical_instances.description: ETHE redemptions were the main reason aggregate ETH ETF net flows ran negative for weeks despite strong inflows into ETHA/FETH, capping ETH's launch. The deeper decline into Aug 5 was amplified by the broad yen carry-trade unwind crash, not by ETHE outflows alone.

price_impacts.window: 2024-07-22 close $3,449 to 2024-08-05 close $2,227 (incl. Aug 5 carry-trade crash)

historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ETHE outflows dominated net ETH ETF flows for the first several weeks post-launch
